title: Why Adults Can Learn Russian Like Children Do
subtitle: The Science Behind the Method
author: The Russian Method
description: A science-based explanation of why traditional language learning fails and how the immersion approach replicates the way children naturally acquire language. Includes neuroscience research and practical principles.

title: Trial Lesson — Lesson 1: Love
subtitle: Your First Step Into Real Russian · $5
author: The Russian Method
description: Everything included in the trial lesson: professional audio narration, bilingual lesson text with language toggle, and a step-by-step practice assignment. Learn at your own pace, any time.

title: Lesson 1 — Love
subtitle: Любовь · First Immersion Lesson
author: Alexander
description: First lesson of The Russian Method. Audio narration by a professional speaker, bilingual text with Russian/English toggle. Features Oleg and Maria in a warm dialogue built around the word любовь — Love.

title: Practice Assignment — Lesson 1
subtitle: 5 Steps to Automatic Speech
author: Alexander
description: Step-by-step practice guide for Lesson 1. From first listening through simultaneous repetition to a final self-check. Designed to move Russian from conscious effort to automatic, subconscious speech.

title: Distinct Cortical Areas Associated with Native and Second Languages
subtitle: Original fMRI Research — Nature, 1997
author: Kim, Relkin, Lee & Hirsch
description: The original Nature paper demonstrating via fMRI that Broca's area contains spatially distinct regions for native and second languages acquired in adulthood.

title: When an Adult Adds a Language
subtitle: It's One Brain, Two Systems
author: Sandra Blakeslee
description: A groundbreaking article explaining how second languages learned in adulthood occupy a separate region of the brain compared to languages acquired in infancy.
